summary: Replaced the remaining `#pragma once` header guards in nine headers with conventional project-style `#ifndef`/`#define` include guards and closing `#endif`s. The touched files were mostly fighter character headers plus `src/melee/sc/scene.h`. This was a mechanical cleanup that standardized header protection across the codebase rather than changing declarations or behavior.
what_changed: Removed `#pragma once` from all touched headers.
Added explicit include guards derived from the full header path, such as `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTDONKEY_FTDONKEY_H`, `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTYOSHI_FTYOSHI_H`, and `MELEE_SC_SCENE_H`.
Added matching closing `#endif` lines at the end of each header.
In `src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftyoshi.h`, the diff also removed one blank line between `#include <melee/lb/lbvector.h>` and the following `sysdolphin` includes while adding the guard.
smart_moves: Kept the PR small and mechanical: no function prototypes, structs, or includes were meaningfully changed beyond header guard boilerplate.
Used deterministic guard macro names that encode the full project-relative include path, reducing collision risk across many character headers with similar filenames.
Converted all remaining instances in one sweep, making future searches for `#pragma once` a simple validation check.
decomp_lessons: Prefer traditional include guards over `#pragma once` when the project style requires portable, explicit header protection.
For decompilation cleanup PRs, isolate style-only or infrastructure-only changes from matching or symbol work so reviewers can verify that no behavior-relevant declarations changed.
Path-derived include guard macros are useful in a large codebase with repeated subsystem naming patterns, especially character-specific headers under `ft/chara`.
naming_conventions: Header guard format observed: uppercase project-relative path components joined by underscores and ending in `_H`.
The guard macro preserves the existing directory/file spelling convention, including character folder/file names such as `FTSEAK`, `FTPURIN`, and `FTZELDA`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactics are evidenced; this PR is a header-guard normalization only.
The safe tactic here was to avoid touching declarations, struct layouts, inline logic, or include order except for the minimal guard insertion.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR had one review, but no review body or actionable feedback was included in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Search the repository for any future reintroduction of `#pragma once`.
Check whether new headers follow the path-derived include guard convention ending in `_H`.
Compare later header cleanup PRs for consistent guard naming in nested directories.
